original_abstract: Diniyah education serves as a cornerstone of the educational landscape in Muslim-majority nations, representing a strategic focal point for policies aimed at harmonizing formal schooling with the reinforcement of religious principles. This article explores the historical trajectory, central role, and evolution of Diniyah education policy. By integrating these programs into national educational frameworks, early religious schooling plays a vital role in shaping theological understanding, ethical conduct, and religious identity. The study highlights how these policies not only elevate the quality of formal education but also foster moral fortitude within the broader community. Despite persistent implementation challenges, the findings suggest that supporting Diniyah initiatives is essential for cultivating a cohesive society rooted in spiritual and ethical values.
humanized_abstract: Diniyah education is a cornerstone of the educational systems in the majority of the Muslim countries. This policy area is a strategic focus area for the alignment of formal education with the promotion of religious education. Diniyah education policy has a long history and central place in early religious education of children with the objective of developing their religious knowledge, moral values and religious identity. It also has the benefits of enhancing the quality of formal education and promoting the moral fibre of the society at large. Although there are numerous constraints in the implementation of the policy, the study opines that a supportive policy for Diniyah education is very crucial for the promotion of a value based and religious conscious society.
